title: Build a Hermes Knowledge Base That Self-Improves
summary: A workflow combining Obsidian, Claude, NotebookLM, and Granola to create a self-improving knowledge base for AI agents like Hermes. The system ingests meeting notes, emails, and research through automated cron jobs and indexing, enabling persistent memory across sessions using LLM wiki principles.
writeup: Jack Roberts walks through a workflow that links Obsidian, Claude, NotebookLM, and Granola into a self-improving knowledge base for AI agents such as Hermes. The system ingests meeting notes, emails, and research through automated cron jobs and indexing, building persistent memory that carries across sessions using LLM wiki principles. It draws on RAG, knowledge graphs, and automated ingestion to keep the store current, and it covers agent evaluation and test-time evaluation as part of the setup.
